perturbation.original_text: STW shall calculate the amount owed to COFS every one hundred-eighty (180) days ("Accrual Period"), and shall provide required payments to COFS within thirty (30) days after the end of the previous Accrual Period.
perturbation.changed_text: STW shall calculate the amount owed to COFS every one hundred-eighty (180) days ("Accrual Period"), and shall provide required payments to COFS within ninety (90) days after the end of the previous Accrual Period.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment deadline from thirty (30) days to ninety (90) days after the Accrual Period. This directly conflicts with the original deadline, creating uncertainty about which term should be enforced and potentially leading to disputes over timely payments. The thirty (30) day deadline is the original agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations if Post-Well Study shows that the Critical Criterion has been met: If all necessary regulatory approvals are obtained that allow the production and transport of up to 10 million GPD or the maximum capacity of the well, for STW's use or resale, in conjunction with STW exercising its Option to Produce Water under Section 15 of this AGREEMENT, STW will pay COFS an upfront Bonus Payment of Five Million and 00/100 Dollars ($5,000,000.00), and STW shall have the immediate right to develop Additional Wells on the Property, subject to all necessary regulatory approvals, in the event STW procures additional water supply contracts that require additional volume of production, the feasibility of which shall be by a Post Well hydrogeological study and report that shows the ability of STW to produce the additional amounts of CRA water. STW shall have the right to the sale of and transport of such additional water produced from Additional Wells. STW shall pay COFS a seventeen percent (17%) royalty of the price STW receives per 1,000 gallons of CRA water produced from the Property and sold. STW shall also provide to COFS three (3) million GPD of CRA water at no cost to COFS at such time that COFS requests delivery of the water for COFS use. Each payment made by STW to COFS will include an accounting statement providing the number of gallons sold, the price paid by any customer(s), the gross revenues received by STW, and the expenses paid by STW for the sale, treatment, use, and/or delivery of the CRA water. STW shall calculate the amount owed to COFS every one hundred-eighty (180) days ("Accrual Period"), and shall provide required payments to COFS within ninety (90) days after the end of the previous Accrual Period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment deadline from thirty (30) days to ninety (90) days, which directly contradicts the requirement within the contradicted text that states STW shall provide required payments to COFS within ninety (90) days after the end of the previous Accrual Period. Although the changed text and contradicted text are in the same location, the change from the original text introducing the new term creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: COFS may not terminate the AGREEMENT during the: a. Pre-well study (ies), b. completion of improvements to the Existing CRA Well, c. completion of the Replacement CRA Well, if necessary, and d. Post-Well Study (ies).
perturbation.changed_text: COFS may not terminate the AGREEMENT during the: a. Pre-well study (ies), b. completion of improvements to the Existing CRA Well, c. completion of the Replacement CRA Well, if necessary, and d. Post-Well Study (ies). COFS can only terminate this agreement if STW is in default.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding COFS's termination rights. The original clause states COFS cannot terminate during specific project phases. The added sentence suggests COFS *can* terminate if STW is in default, implying the earlier restriction is not absolute and creating ambiguity. The phrase: 'COFS may not terminate the AGREEMENT during the:' creates the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: STW shall have the right to unilaterally terminate the provisions of this AGREEMENT related only to the Property, and not proceed further after the completion of any phase of the project and not incur any additional costs. So long as STW is progressing through the following phases, and subject to the other terms of this AGREEMENT, COFS may not terminate the AGREEMENT during the: a. Pre-well study (ies), b. completion of improvements to the Existing CRA Well, c. completion of the Replacement CRA Well, if necessary, and d. Post-Well Study (ies). COFS can only terminate this agreement if STW is in default. Should COFS unilaterally terminate the provisions of this AGREEMENT related to COFS Property only, with the right to do so beginning on a period beginning no sooner than ninety (90) days following the Post-Well Study (ies), and ending at the one year period following the Post-Well Study (ies), during which STW may exercise its Option to Produce Water, in accordance with Section 15 of this AGREEMENT, COFS shall reimburse STW for 100% of the costs paid by STW up to the point of such termination, pursuant to the AGREEMENT, according to a reasonable accounting schedule of costs prepared by STW and submitted to COFS.
perturbation.justification: The original text states COFS may not terminate the agreement during specific phases of the project. The changed text adds 'COFS can only terminate this agreement if STW is in default.' This creates a direct contradiction because the original text implies an absolute restriction on COFS's termination rights during those phases, while the added sentence provides an exception, thus creating an inconsistency. The contradictory text is: 'So long as STW is progressing through the following phases, and subject to the other terms of this AGREEMENT, COFS may not terminate the AGREEMENT during the: a. Pre-well study (ies), b. completion of improvements to the Existing CRA Well, c. completion of the Replacement CRA Well, if necessary, and d. Post-Well Study (ies). COFS can only terminate this agreement if STW is in default.' This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.
